Article title: Another U.S. strike in Caribbean targets alleged drug-running boat, killing 6, Hegseth says
LEFT: The United States conducted another military strike in the Caribbean against an alleged drug-running vessel, resulting in the deaths of six people onboard. Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth announced the strike, stating it occurred Friday morning in international waters and was the "first strike at night." Hegseth claimed the vessel was involved in illicit narcotics smuggling, transiting a known route, and operated by Tren de Aragua (TdA), which he called a "Designated Terrorist Organization." No U.S. forces were harmed. The strikes have drawn condemnation from civil liberties groups and several South American countries, including Colombian President Gustavo Petro, who described them as "murder."
CENTER: The U.S. military conducted its tenth strike on a suspected drug-running boat in the Caribbean overnight, resulting in six deaths. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced the operation, attributing the vessel to the Tren de Aragua gang. This strike, which Hegseth stated occurred in international waters and was the first conducted at night, is part of an accelerated pace of U.S. military actions, with three strikes this week, including two in the eastern Pacific. These operations occur amidst a U.S. military buildup near Venezuela and have drawn concerns from lawmakers over the lack of congressional authorization for such actions.

Article title: Chelsea Clinton Laments Trump Demeans the White House with His Renovations
RIGHT: Chelsea Clinton criticized President Donald Trump's White House renovations, including the demolition of part of the East Wing for a new ballroom, in a USA Today op-ed. She accused Trump of a "disregard for history" and taking a "wrecking ball to our heritage," advocating for stewardship over mere authority. Trump's administration and supporters countered, with Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt noting "nearly every single president" has made renovations. The new ballroom, touted as "world-class," is reportedly privately funded by Trump and corporate sponsors, with an estimated cost of $250 million. Critics of Clinton invoked past scandals involving her family's White House tenure.

Article title: East Wing of White House torn down as Trump clears space for his ballroom
LEFT: The East Wing of the White House has been completely demolished to make way for President Donald Trump’s planned $300m gilded ballroom. Satellite images confirmed the historic section was reduced to rubble, contradicting Trump’s earlier promise in July that no existing infrastructure would be torn down. The demolition proceeded without prior submission of plans to the National Capital Planning Commission (NCPC), although White House officials stated permits for vertical construction would be submitted later. Historians and a non-profit organization contend that demolition plans legally require public review. A Virginia couple has filed a motion for a temporary restraining order, alleging the project bypasses legally required approvals.
CENTER: The East Wing and East Colonnade of the White House have been demolished as part of President Trump’s plan to construct a new ballroom, initially proposed for 650 people and later expanded to 1,000. This demolition, initiated without prior public notice, has drawn significant criticism from preservationists and historians. The National Trust for Historic Preservation voiced concerns that the new construction would "overwhelm the White House itself." While the White House defended the renovation, stating the East Wing would become "more beautiful and modern," critics highlight this as the first major change to the building's exterior in 80 years. The White House is exempt from standard historic preservation review processes.

Article title: ESPN's Stephen A. Smith Falsely Claims NBA Gambling Investigation is Trump’s ‘Revenge’
RIGHT: ESPN's Stephen A. Smith falsely claimed on "First Take" that an FBI investigation into illegal NBA gambling was Donald Trump's "revenge" against the league. Smith speculated Trump, with his casino ties, was "coming" for the NBA and potentially the WNBA, citing an FBI director's press conference as unusual. However, the investigation, reportedly named "Operation Royal Flush," began in 2022 under the Biden administration. FBI Director Kash Patel dismissed Smith's assertion as "the single dumbest thing I've ever heard." The probe led to 31 arrests, including Portland Trail Blazers coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier, and is linked to organized crime.

Article title: Feds: Games Involving the Lakers, Raptors, Hornets, Trail Blazers, Included in NBA Gambling Scheme
RIGHT: Federal authorities have uncovered an illegal gambling and sports rigging scheme spanning January 2022 to March 2024, leading to over 30 arrests, including NBA figures Terry Rozier, Damon Jones, and Chauncey Billups. The scheme allegedly involved sharing non-public information, such as player injuries and game inactivity, for fraudulent prop bets. Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier is accused of faking an injury in a March 2023 game while with the Hornets, enabling friends to bet over $200,000 on his prop bets. Former NBA player Damon Jones allegedly leaked information about LeBron James's injury before a February 2023 Lakers game, prompting bets on the opposing team. The operation is linked to organized crime and involved games with the Lakers, Raptors, Hornets, and Trail Blazers.

Article title: Nancy Pelosi: Local Police May Arrest Federal Agents for 'Excessive Use of Force'
RIGHT: Speaker Emerita Nancy Pelosi stated that local authorities could arrest federal agents if they break state laws, particularly regarding "excessive use of force." San Francisco District Attorney Brooke Jenkins outlined a plan to identify agents through camera footage, seek warrants, and prosecute. In response, top federal officials, including Attorney General Pam Bondi and Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, warned Pelosi, California Governor Gavin Newsom, Illinois Governor JB Pritzker, and DA Jenkins against interfering with federal agents. They asserted that federal law enforcement would not tolerate threats or arrests of agents enforcing immigration laws, threatening legal action for obstruction. Legal experts note limited precedent for states to prosecute federal officers acting within legal bounds.

Article title: New York Attorney General Letitia James enters plea in federal mortgage fraud case
LEFT: New York Attorney General Letitia James pleaded not guilty in federal court in Virginia to charges of bank fraud and false statements. The indictment alleges James falsely claimed a home purchased in Norfolk, Virginia, in 2020 for $137,000 would be a secondary residence to secure favorable loan terms, while allegedly using it as an investment property. The charges, brought by Trump's appointed US attorney Lindsey Halligan, reportedly faced objections from career prosecutors who cited insufficient evidence and James not materially profiting. James's defense argues the case is politically motivated, following public calls for her prosecution by Donald Trump, whom James previously investigated.
RIGHT: New York Attorney General Letitia James was arraigned in a federal court in Norfolk, Virginia, where she pleaded not guilty to two felony charges of bank fraud and making false statements to a financial institution. The charges stem from her 2020 purchase of a home in Norfolk, Virginia. Prosecutors allege James misrepresented the property as a secondary residence to secure a lower interest rate, instead renting it out to a family, potentially saving nearly $19,000 over a 30-year loan. James called the charges "baseless" and "political retribution," asserting the justice system is being weaponized. A trial date has been set for January 26, 2026.
CENTER: New York Attorney General Letitia James is set to appear in court on federal charges of bank fraud and making false statements to a financial institution. The indictment stems from a 2020 home purchase in Norfolk, Virginia, where James allegedly signed a "second home rider" agreeing to personal use but then rented out the property, securing favorable loan terms. James, a Democrat who has frequently sued former President Trump, denies wrongdoing, calling the indictment a "weaponization of our justice system." Her indictment followed the resignation of the U.S. attorney who resisted pressure to charge her, and the appointment of a new prosecutor whose conduct and appointment are being challenged by James's defense team.

Article title: Rubio slams UNRWA as a 'subsidiary of Hamas,' vows it will not 'play any role' in delivering aid to Gaza
LEFT: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, during a visit to Israel, stated that Hamas "cannot be involved in governing Gaza in the future." Speaking from the US-Israel ceasefire coordination centre in Kiryat Gat, Rubio also asserted that the UN agency for Palestinian refugees, UNRWA, "cannot play a role in Gaza," describing it as a "subsidiary of Hamas." He further added that Israel must be "comfortable" with the composition of any international force in Gaza. These remarks underscore the US position on future governance and aid delivery in the region.
RIGHT: Secretary of State Marco Rubio declared that the United Nations Relief and Works Agency (UNRWA) would "not play any role" in delivering aid to Gaza, labeling it a "subsidiary of Hamas." This position follows President Donald Trump's February reaffirmation of the U.S. commitment to defund UNRWA, amid allegations of its employees' involvement in the October 7, 2023, Hamas attack. An UNRWA investigation into 19 staff members led to nine firings over evidence of potential involvement. The U.S. Central Command established a Civil-Military Coordination Center in southern Israel to manage Gaza stabilization and aid. UN Secretary-General António Guterres, conversely, has defended UNRWA as "vital to any prospects for peace."

Article title: Sex offender who sparked UK asylum hotel protests released by mistake
LEFT: Hadush Gerberslasie Kebatu, an Ethiopian national, 38, who was jailed for sexually assaulting a woman and a 14-year-old girl near an Epping asylum hotel, has been accidentally released from jail. Kebatu, whose actions previously sparked nationwide protests, was meant to be transferred to an immigration detention centre for deportation after serving his 12-month sentence, but was freed by mistake. Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy is reportedly furious about the error and has ordered an investigation, while also supporting police efforts to locate Kebatu.
CENTER: Hadush Gerberslasie Kebatu, an Ethiopian national and asylum seeker, was mistakenly released from HMP Chelmsford despite being sentenced to 12 months for sexually assaulting a 14-year-old girl and a woman. Kebatu, whose attacks sparked nationwide protests, was meant to be transferred to an immigration detention centre for planned deportation after his September sentencing. He was also given a five-year sexual harm prevention order and made to sign the Sex Offenders Register for ten years. Police have launched a manhunt to find him, while the Prison Service has initiated an investigation into the error, leading to an officer's removal from duties. Justice Secretary David Lammy expressed his dismay, stating Kebatu must be deported.

Article title: Trump Ends Trade Talks with Canada over 'Fraudulent' anti-Tariff TV Ads
LEFT: President Donald Trump announced the termination of all trade negotiations with Canada, citing a "fraudulent" anti-tariff television advertisement run by the Canadian province of Ontario. The ad features excerpts from a 1987 speech by former President Ronald Reagan criticizing trade barriers. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed the suspension, while the Reagan Foundation stated Ontario "misrepresents" Reagan's remarks and used them without permission. Ontario Premier Doug Ford defended the ad, emphasizing US-Canada ties. This dispute occurs amidst existing US tariffs on Canadian goods and an upcoming US Supreme Court hearing on Trump's tariff authority.
RIGHT: President Donald Trump terminated trade negotiations with Canada, accusing the province of Ontario of airing a "fraudulent" anti-tariff television advertisement. Trump claimed the ad, which quoted former President Ronald Reagan, "misrepresents" Reagan's views on tariffs. The Ronald Reagan Foundation confirmed the ad used "selective" audio and video to distort his remarks and was reviewing legal options. Trump had previously imposed import tariffs on Canadian steel, aluminum, and autos. Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney indicated plans to diversify exports due to tariff threats. Nearly US$2.7 billion in goods and services cross the U.S.-Canada border daily, with over 75% of Canadian exports going to the U.S.
CENTER: President Donald Trump announced the termination of "all trade negotiations" with Canada, citing a Canadian television ad opposing U.S. tariffs. Trump labeled the ad "fraudulent" and "fake," claiming it misstated facts and aimed to influence U.S. Supreme Court decisions on tariffs. The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute confirmed the Ontario government's ad "misrepresents" Reagan's 1987 address and was used without permission. This action follows Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's earlier statement about aiming to double non-U.S. exports due to tariff threats, further escalating existing trade tensions between the two nations.

Article title: U.S. inflation stays elevated but prices rose less than feared last month
RIGHT: U.S. consumer prices increased less than expected in September, rising 0.3 percent, which was below the 0.4 percent forecast by economists and a decrease from August's 0.4 percent. Annually, consumer prices are up 3.0 percent. Core prices, excluding volatile food and energy, also rose less than expected at 0.2 percent for the month and are up 3.0 percent over the past year. Food inflation cooled, with grocery prices climbing 0.3 percent, while energy prices were a significant contributor, increasing 1.5 percent in September. Shelter prices saw a 0.2 percent rise, contributing to an annual gain of 3.6 percent.
CENTER: U.S. consumer prices in September showed annual inflation at 3.0%, a figure slightly below economists' forecasts, although still elevated above the Federal Reserve's 2% target. On a monthly basis, prices increased by 0.3%, a slight cooling from the 0.4% rise in August. Core inflation, excluding food and energy, also saw a modest decline. The report, delayed by the government shutdown, indicated that gas prices jumped 4.1% in September, while rental and some service costs moderated. This data will likely reinforce expectations for the Federal Reserve to implement another interest rate cut. The figures are also crucial for calculating the 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for Social Security recipients next year.

Article title: WATCH: Kash Patel Reveals Details of Massive 'Sports Rigging' Op Rocking the NBA
RIGHT: FBI Director Kash Patel announced the arrest of 34 individuals, including NBA coach Chauncey Billups, active player Terry Rozier, and former player/coach Damon Jones, as part of a widespread investigation into illegal gambling and sports rigging. The probe is linked to La Cosa Nostra crime families, including the Bonanno, Gambino, and Genovese. Billups and Jones are implicated in rigged poker games, with Billups allegedly used to attract victims. Rozier is accused of faking an injury in a March 2023 game to facilitate "under" prop bets, while Jones allegedly leaked non-public information about a prominent Lakers player's injury. The NBA placed Billups and Rozier on immediate leave, emphasizing the integrity of the game.

Article title: ‘I Am Coming for Your Seats’ — Exclusive: Scott Presler Says We Must Win in November or Left Will Have ‘Gotten Away with Murder’ of Charlie Kirk
RIGHT: Early Vote Action founder Scott Presler appeared on “The Alex Marlow Show,” urging Republicans to ensure high turnout in the upcoming November elections. Presler stated that if Republicans do not win, “the very people who celebrated Charlie Kirk’s assassination win” will prevail. He proposed a strategy for supporters to text 32 people, commemorating what would have been Charlie Kirk's 32nd birthday on October 14, aiming to mobilize Republican voters for 2025. “The Alex Marlow Show” is a weekday podcast hosted by Breitbart Editor-in-Chief Alex Marlow and produced by Breitbart News and Salem Podcast Network.

Article title: ‘Vicious and frenzied’: the fatal attack on Walsall hotel worker Rhiannon Whyte
LEFT: Rhiannon Skye Whyte, a 27-year-old hotel worker, was fatally attacked on October 20, 2024, after finishing her shift at the Park Inn hotel in Wolverhampton. Deng Chol Majek, an asylum seeker residing at the same hotel, followed Whyte to Bescot Stadium railway station, where he stabbed her 23 times with a screwdriver. Whyte died three days later in hospital from a fatal brain injury. Majek was subsequently found guilty of murder at Wolverhampton Crown Court. Evidence presented included CCTV footage of Majek following Whyte and later dancing, alongside Whyte's DNA found on his belongings and under his fingernails.
CENTER: Deng Chol Majek, an asylum seeker, has been found guilty of murdering 27-year-old hotel worker Rhiannon Whyte in Walsall. Majek, who was staying at the Park Inn Hotel where Ms. Whyte worked, stabbed her 23 times with a screwdriver at Bescot Stadium station in October 2024. CCTV captured Majek following Ms. Whyte, fleeing the scene, and later dancing at the hotel, behavior prosecutors described as "callous." Despite DNA and CCTV evidence, Majek denied the killing, claiming the evidence was incorrect. Ms. Whyte died three days after the attack, and no motive for the "vicious and frenzied" assault was ever established. An age assessment has been ordered for Majek before his sentencing.
